text.title: Trend Analysis Agentic Pipeline
text.summary: Fan-out/fan-in subagent architecture, prompt patterns for orchestrator and counter-signal critic, and operational gotchas.

rule.statement: Run trend analysis as a fan-out/fan-in pipeline: one orchestrator dispatches per-source collectors in parallel; a synthesizer normalizes them into STEEP and Trend-Strength tables; a counter-signal critic runs before the recommendation is written.
rule.rationale: Serial collection is slow. The counter-signal critic is non-negotiable — a bullish-only pipeline will recommend "pursue" on every trend regardless of underlying strength.

list.item: faion-market-researcher-agent: orchestrator, owns STEEP + scoring rubric and final report
web-search-collector (Haiku): runs WebSearch/WebFetch against signal sources, returns JSON rows only — no reasoning
data-quantifier (Sonnet): converts raw source dumps into Google Trends deltas, GitHub star-velocity, funding totals
counter-signal-critic (Sonnet): adversarial agent — searches for cooling indicators, failed precedents, regulatory blockers
personal-fit-scorer (Haiku): applies the user's skill/audience/interest filter from a memory file

example.note: Orchestrator prompt — strict JSON schema with source_url required field:
example.code: You are the trend-analysis orchestrator. Analyze trend "{TREND}". Output ONLY this JSON schema: { "trend": "...", "category": "fad|trend|megatrend|shift", "stage": "innovator|early|mainstream|decline", "evidence": [{"metric":"","value":"","yoy":"","source_url":""}], "steep": {"social":"","tech":"","econ":"","env":"","political":""}, "score": {"growth":1-5,"stage":1-5,"incumbents":1-5,"investment":1-5,"fit":1-5,"weighted_total":0-5}, "counter_signals": ["..."], "recommendation": "pursue|monitor|skip", "next_review": "YYYY-MM-DD" } Rules: every numeric field MUST cite source_url. If unknown, write "unknown" — never estimate.

example.note: Counter-signal critic prompt — adversarial, returns 3-7 bullets with source URLs:
example.code: You are the counter-signal critic. Given trend "{TREND}" and the prior bullish report, search for: (a) declining adjacent metrics, (b) regulatory threats, (c) prior cycle failures with same shape, (d) incumbent moats that prevent entry. Return 3-7 bullets, each with a source URL.

rule.statement: Run trend analysis monthly on a recurring cadence — first run alone is near-useless. Trend strength is read from the slope across runs.
rule.rationale: A single snapshot cannot distinguish a spike from a trend. Monthly deltas reveal acceleration or cooling. First-run data is baseline only.

rule.statement: Apply the personal-fit filter before scoring, not after. Filter 100 trends to 10 by fit, then score the 10.
rule.rationale: Scoring 100 trends and rejecting on fit at the end wastes 90% of scoring compute and tempts the agent to recommend fit-mismatched trends anyway.

rule.statement: Enforce a two-week sanity gap — never act on a trend the same session it was discovered. Log it, re-evaluate on the next dashboard run.
rule.rationale: Recency bias causes immediate action on trends that cool within 2 weeks. The gap filters ephemeral spikes from sustained signals.

list.item: Numeric hallucination: LLMs invent CAGRs and funding totals. Force JSON schema with source_url required; reject rows without it.
Stale training data: model reports 2023 funding rounds as current. Always wire WebSearch/WebFetch and check current_date against returned URLs.
STEEP padding: agents fill all five cells even when Environmental is irrelevant. Accept "n/a" as valid.
Recommendation drift toward "pursue": default LLM tone is optimistic. Counter-agent + forced skip budget (reject >= 40% of trends per run) counteract this.
Date-window mismatch: one source returns 30-day, another YoY. Normalize windows in the collector layer.
Token blowup: never paste full HTML/JSON into the synthesizer — collectors must return <= 20-row distilled records.

text.reference: Trend Analysis Report with evidence table, STEEP, and recommendation sections.
Multi-source collector: Google Trends, HN, GitHub.
